summary.intent: Fix GitHub rate limiting issues in the ocp4-scan-konflux Jenkins job that was degraded even after removing GitHub App ID and falling back to GITHUB_TOKEN. Understand why rate limiting persisted and fix the root cause to enable proper GitHub App authentication.
summary.outcome: Identified that PR #2587 (GitHub App auth support) missed updating source_resolver.py methods that were making unauthenticated git operations, hitting the 60 req/hr anonymous limit instead of 5,000/hr authenticated limit. PR #2708 reverted the changes; created a plan to re-land with fixes but implementation was not completed in this session.

repo: The ocp4-scan-konflux job clones hundreds of repos and performs git ls-remote for each image, easily exceeding low rate limits
Multiple concurrent OCP version scan jobs share the same GitHub PAT rate limit pool (5,000/hr total)
GitHub App installations provide separate 5,000/hr buckets per org (openshift-eng, openshift-priv, openshift), effectively ~15,000/hr total
The codebase converts SSH URLs to HTTPS for authentication via GIT_ASKPASS but PR #2587 didn't update all call sites
Jenkins configures SSH agent (openshift-bot) for backward compatibility but new code prefers HTTPS with tokens

code.finding: setup_and_fetch_public_upstream_source() only passed GIT_NO_PROMPTS to git fetch, making unauthenticated requests to public upstream repos (60/hr limit)
_get_remote_branch_ref() runs git ls-remote with no set_env at all, relying only on SSH agent instead of token auth
_check_branch_protection() uses raw GITHUB_TOKEN instead of get_github_client_for_org() which resolves the correct App installation per org
get_github_git_auth_env(url=...) extracts org from URL and selects correct installation token, enabling per-org rate limit buckets
Commit 3e3153e separated _local_env() from _git_env_for_remote() to avoid calling get_github_git_auth_env() for local-only git operations that don't contact remotes

workflow: GitHub anonymous git operations hit 60 req/hr limit; authenticated operations get 5,000/hr per token/installation
GitHub App auth is better than PAT for multi-org workloads since each installation gets separate rate limit buckets
Jenkins console logs reveal actual git commands and environment variables, useful for diagnosing auth issues
Git operations over HTTPS and REST API calls share the same rate limit pool when using the same token
Rate limiting can manifest as slow operations or timeouts rather than explicit 429 errors

friction: PR #2587 was incomplete - added new auth infrastructure but missed updating source_resolver.py git operation call sites
Rate limiting issues weren't caught in testing, only discovered after merge when production jobs degraded
The rate limiting manifested as performance degradation rather than clear errors, making root cause analysis difficult
Had to revert the entire PR #2708 instead of forward-fixing due to severity of production impact
Understanding the issue required tracing through 5+ layers: Jenkinsfile → artcd → doozer → source_resolver → git_helper → github_auth
open_items: Implement the fixes: re-land PR #2587 changes and add auth to source_resolver.py methods (setup_and_fetch_public_upstream_source, _get_remote_branch_ref, _check_branch_protection)
Push new branch to origin and raise PR to prod with the complete fixes
Test that fixes resolve rate limiting with both GITHUB_TOKEN and GitHub App auth in production
Consider adding GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID env vars per-org to Jenkins jobs to skip installation lookup API overhead
Monitor for secondary GitHub abuse detection rate limits that can trigger on rapid request bursts regardless of auth method
